skill.name: drizzle-orm-patterns
skill.description: Use Drizzle ORM patterns for PostgreSQL, migrations, transactions, relations, performance, type inference, and Vercel Edge Runtime deployments.
skill.summary: Reference Drizzle ORM patterns for safe, type-safe PostgreSQL work in edge and server runtimes.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Manual review found that the static analyzer mostly flagged Markdown code fences, Drizzle SQL template examples, official documentation links, and validator detection patterns. The only executable file is validate-queries.py, which reads caller-supplied TypeScript files and prints local findings; no autonomous network calls, shell execution, credential exfiltration, or prompt-injection text was found. Publish with a low-risk note that users should run the validator only on intended repository paths.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution alerts are documentation false positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell and Ruby backtick patterns occur in Markdown bash fences, inline examples, and Drizzle sql template examples. The executable validator does not import subprocess, os.system, popen, exec, eval, or network client libraries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documentation or string-matching rules, and no executable shell launch API was found in validate-queries.py.

low_findings.title: Environment and network alerts are normal examples and reference links
low_findings.description: DATABASE_URL appears in setup examples for Drizzle and Neon. Hardcoded URLs are official documentation links, and no reviewed code sends environment values to an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is database configuration guidance and resource links, not hidden outbound communication or credential handling.

low_findings.title: Critical obfuscation heuristic is a benign hashing example
low_findings.description: The fromCharCode and btoa sequence converts a SHA-256 digest to a response string inside an edge runtime example. It is visible documentation code and is not paired with credential collection or outbound network transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequence is explained by adjacent Web Crypto API code and has no evidence of concealment or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Validator accepts caller-provided file paths
low_findings.description: The Python validator expands CLI glob patterns and validates .ts or .tsx files selected by the user. This is expected for a local code audit tool, but users should run it only on intended repository paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access behavior is real, but it is explicit CLI behavior with no autonomous discovery, network transfer, or secret parsing.

low_findings.title: Prompt injection indicators were not found
low_findings.description: Manual keyword review did not find instructions to ignore prior rules, override system behavior, skip security review, claim pre-approval, or assume elevated authority.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only keyword matches were ordinary comments using the word forget, not instruction-hijacking text.

content.value_statement: Database code can become unsafe, slow, or hard to maintain without clear Drizzle patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical references for PostgreSQL queries, migrations, transactions, relations, and edge deployments.
actual_capabilities: Provides Drizzle query patterns for selects, filters, joins, inserts, updates, deletes, aggregations, subqueries, CTEs, and unions.
Explains transaction patterns, rollback behavior, isolation levels, retry logic, locking, and deadlock prevention.
Documents relation modeling for one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many, self-referencing, circular, and cascading relationships.
Covers migration workflows for schema changes, data migrations, zero-downtime deployment, rollback, and migration testing.
Includes edge runtime guidance for Neon serverless, Vercel Edge, connection pooling, runtime limits, and performance tradeoffs.
Includes a local Python validator for common Drizzle security, performance, transaction, and type-safety issues.
limitations: The examples focus on PostgreSQL and Drizzle ORM, not every database supported by Drizzle.
The validator uses pattern matching and can miss complex data-flow issues or report false positives.
Some examples reference Quetrex architecture and may need adaptation for other projects.
The skill does not run migrations or change databases by itself.

use_cases.title: Design a safe Drizzle schema
use_cases.description: Model PostgreSQL tables, relationships, indexes, and inferred TypeScript types before implementing a new feature.

use_cases.title: Review risky database code
use_cases.description: Check for SQL injection, N+1 queries, missing pagination, weak transaction boundaries, and unsafe migration steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare an edge database deployment
use_cases.description: Adapt Drizzle queries and Neon connections for Vercel Edge Runtime while avoiding unsupported Node.js APIs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the drizzle-orm-patterns skill to write a type-safe Drizzle query for this table and filter. Explain the selected fields, where clause, and limit behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the drizzle-orm-patterns skill to plan a PostgreSQL migration for this schema change. Include rollout steps, rollback steps, and data backfill risks.

prompt_templates.title: Review Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the drizzle-orm-patterns skill to review this Drizzle data access code for N+1 queries, missing indexes, pagination gaps, and inefficient field selection.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Edge Runtime Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the drizzle-orm-patterns skill to evaluate this Vercel Edge Runtime database design. Check connection handling, transaction boundaries, unsupported APIs, caching, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: Review a user lookup query that interpolates an email into raw SQL.
output: Identifies the SQL injection risk and recommends Drizzle comparison operators or parameterized expressions.
Suggests selecting only needed fields and adding a result limit.
Calls out where input validation belongs before the database layer.

output_examples.input: Plan a migration that adds a required column to a large users table.
output: Proposes a staged rollout with nullable column creation, backfill, application update, and final constraint enforcement.
Includes rollback considerations and batch processing guidance.
Notes index and lock risks for production databases.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether a Drizzle setup works in Vercel Edge Runtime.
output: Checks that the setup uses an edge-compatible Neon HTTP driver.
Warns against Node.js-only filesystem and native driver patterns.
Recommends short queries, explicit limits, and runtime-aware error handling.

best_practices: Use Drizzle operators and parameterized expressions instead of interpolating user input into raw SQL.
Define indexes, limits, and selected fields intentionally before optimizing application code.
Keep migrations reversible and test rollback paths before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not run long external API calls inside database transactions.
Do not use Node.js filesystem APIs in Vercel Edge Runtime handlers.
Do not depend on the validator as the only security review for database code.

faq.question: What projects is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for TypeScript projects using Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, especially projects using Neon or Vercel Edge Runtime.

faq.question: Does the skill support database migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents schema changes, data migrations, zero-downtime rollout patterns, rollback strategies, and migration testing.

faq.question: Can it prevent SQL injection automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides safe patterns and a local validator, but developers must still review inputs and query construction.

faq.question: Does it execute database commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation plus a local validator script. It does not connect to a database by itself.

faq.question: Is the validator safe to run?
faq.answer: The validator reads TypeScript files that the user supplies and prints local findings. Run it only on intended repository paths.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for applying the documented patterns.
